arguments.task: Research Sentry using official first-party sources only where possible. Open docs.sentry.io/api and extract: one-line product description, API authentication methods for public API and any token formats, rate limit notes, API surface type (REST or GraphQL), breadth of endpoints from docs navigation, and evidence URLs. Then visit official pricing/access pages on sentry.io to determine whether API access is self-serve free, trial, paid, admin approval, or partner gated, with exact evidence. Then search official Sentry docs or GitHub for MCP server availability, prioritizing first-party docs and official GitHub repos. Return concise findings with exact page URLs for each claim. Avoid third-party blogs unless no official source exists for MCP, and note if MCP cannot be verified officially.

Search for an existing MCP for Sentry, prioritizing official Sentry GitHub organization repositories and official docs. Check if Sentry has an official MCP server or documented MCP integration. Capture repository URLs or docs URLs and whether the MCP is official or community-maintained. Return only evidence-backed findings.

input.thought: Launch parallel browser research tasks for official Sentry API/auth/access docs and MCP availability.

arguments.task: Using official Sentry docs only, extract exact text and page URLs for: 1) what authentication methods the Sentry API supports, including whether auth tokens and internal integration tokens are bearer tokens and whether OAuth is supported; 2) whether auth tokens are tied to users versus integrations; 3) whether the API is RESTful; 4) a concise statement of API breadth from the API docs navigation or overview; 5) exact rate limit notes including fixed window and rate-limit headers. Return a short evidence-backed summary with URLs only from docs.sentry.io.

Using official Sentry pricing and official docs only, determine whether Sentry public API access is available on free/self-serve plans or requires a paid plan. Inspect the pricing comparison table row labeled API and the docs about authentication token availability. Return exact evidence-backed finding with URLs, including if there is ambiguity between docs and pricing.

input.thought: Run focused follow-up tasks to resolve API auth and pricing ambiguity with official evidence only.

raw_result.description: Developer-first application monitoring platform with a public web API for managing account resources and exporting data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API auth docs show bearer-token authentication via user auth tokens and internal integration tokens, and also document OAuth2 support for integrations; token scoping differs between user tokens and integration tokens.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page includes a $0 Developer self-serve plan, and gathered evidence indicates API access is included on the Developer plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public web API docs at docs.sentry.io/api cover account, organization, project, issue/event, user, team, release, monitor, and other management/data-export endpoints across a broad platform surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Sentry has an official MCP offering, with docs at mcp.sentry.dev and official getsentry GitHub repositories including sentry-mcp; gathered evidence indicates beta status.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs describe fixed-window limits and response headers including X-Sentry-Rate-Limit-Limit, X-Sentry-Rate-Limit-Remaining, and X-Sentry-Rate-Limit-Reset.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official MCP exists; API docs are public and comprehensive; pricing is self-serve with free and paid tiers; auth supports both bearer tokens and OAuth2, which is favorable for agent/toolkit development.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The browser-gathered pricing evidence indicated API access on the free Developer tier, but an earlier extraction suggested possible ambiguity in the pricing table row interpretation. Access-tier conclusion is evidence-backed but should be spot-checked by a human on the live pricing page.
